mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (currently 4.1%) signals a tight labor market that compresses labor supply and drives up wages, lifting inflation expectations and thus raising the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: If over a rolling 2-year window a decline in UNRATE is associated with falling yields (negative correlation), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production of more than 0.5% signals accelerating factory output and robust economic expansion, which raises growth forecasts and pushes long-term yields higher.
failure_condition: If fewer than 55% of triggered episodes are followed by a 20-trading-day yield increase, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts and retail sales both exceed their respective 12-month moving averages, synchronized demand strength amplifies growth expectations beyond the sum of individual effects, driving the 10-year yield up.
failure_condition: If the joint signal's rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or the interaction term in a linear regression is insignificant (p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2.5%, further inflation increases force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which lifts the 10-year yield through higher policy rate expectations.
failure_condition: If within the high-inflation regime, rising CPI is not followed by a yield increase in more than 60% of observations over a 2-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate (currently 1.15%) signals that the central bank will need to normalize rates over the near term, increasing term premium and pushing the 10-year yield higher.
failure_condition: If over a rolling 2-year window lower FEDFUNDS levels are associated with declining 10-year yields, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 indicate strong labor demand that accelerates wage growth and underlying inflation, causing the 10-year yield to rise.
failure_condition: If fewer than 55% of threshold crossings are followed by a yield increase over the next 20 trading days, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises (CPIAUCSL monthly growth >0.2%) and unemployment falls (UNRATE monthly decline >0.1%), the combination signals an overheating economy that compels the Fed to tighten aggressively, lifting yields more than either factor alone.
failure_condition: If the joint signal's Sharpe ratio over triggered events is below 0.5 or the interaction coefficient in a regression of yield changes on the two series is insignificant (p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where retail sales (RSAFS) exceed 500,000 (high consumer spending), further increases in RSAFS signal persistent demand strength that boosts GDP growth and raises the 10-year yield.
failure_condition: If in the high-consumption regime (RSAFS >500k), rising RSAFS is followed by yield declines in more than 40% of cases over a 2-year window, reject.
